text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
I'm guessing you've come to volunteer yourself.
You must be willing to become my test subject, as
a gift for my birthday.

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text:
I thought you'd say that. But I've got a normal
present for you... so enough of that.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
Ahahaha! Not only can you see invisible people,
it seems you can predict the future now too.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
Hmm, when your occipital lobe processes visual
information, your limbic system becomes active,
as if visualizing something.

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
That region may be more developed in you
compared to other subjects.

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text:
Are you trying to say... that the world I see is...
some kind of hallucination?

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
No! You can perceive phenomena that science is
unable to prove the existence of. You possess a
rare specimen of a brain!

text_block_list.name: Agnes Tachyon
text_block_list.text:
Have you grown curious to learn more about your
mind?! We can start with a simple brain wave
scan! Just let me fetch some electrodes and a
respirator!

text_block_list.name: Manhattan Cafe
text_block_list.text: ...No, thanks. Also, I forgot to tell you something.
